overall.description: A ship simulator game showing a first-person view from the vessel's bridge at night. The camera faces forward over the ship's bow with navigation monitors and radar displays visible overhead, while the vessel moves steadily through dark waters.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge navigation station with forward deck view

agents.ego_description: piloting the vessel from the bridge while monitoring the radar display and navigation charts

entered.detail: navigation display showing coastal chart with depth contours and waypoints
circular radar display with rotating yellow sweep indicating surrounding vessel contacts
blue digital display showing vessel telemetry and navigation data
forward deck of the ship with metal plating and anchor equipment visible through the window
white metal safety railing along the port side of the forecastle deck
white metal safety railing along the starboard side of the forecastle deck
dark water surface illuminated by the ship's forward lighting

segments.scene_description: The view from a ship's bridge at night, looking forward over the vessel's bow. Three navigation monitors are mounted overhead displaying charts, radar, and ship data. The ship moves steadily through dark waters while the radar sweep rotates continuously, with the bow deck and safety railings visible through the forward windows.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot maintains a steady first-person perspective from a ship's bridge, capturing a constant forward progression through a dark nocturnal seascape. It begins with a view over the vessel's bow, where the safety railings and deck are visible through the forward windows. As the ship moves steadily through the dark waters, the focus remains on the intersection of the physical environment and the digital navigation tools. The scene is defined by the continuous rotation of the radar sweep on the overhead monitors, which display flickering charts and ship data against the surrounding blackness.
